sessions.lever_rule: split-init-accumulation NOT sanctioned (cheat-by-spelling per layer-2 review)

reviewer.verdict: PASS (candidate.c floor-3 baseline) / FAIL (rejected/split-init-off-accumulation.c — both layer-1 2026-06-16 and layer-2 2026-06-22)
reviewer.evidence: The candidate.c floor-3 baseline remains PASS (reassociated integer add, pure C). The split-init-off-accumulation form (rejected/) is FAIL by user decision after layer-2 adversarial review found: (1) the cited SOTN evidence for the broad split-init-accumulation family was mischaracterized — e_coffin.c posX/posY asymmetry is semantic (posY adds a table offset; posX has nothing to add), NOT RA-motivated; (2) src/dra/6BF64.c argY case is inside a for-loop the scan script's 15-line lookback window missed; (3) e_bone_ark.c angle += ROT(360) is angle normalization, not codegen coercion; (4) the cited sp20 example uses a name that announces stack-slot role; (5) the EXACT form for this function was already rejected by layer-1 on 2026-06-16 — sanctioning would be the self-sanctioning antipattern review-discipline-before-commit.md was designed to catch; (6) the 2026-06-13 narrow sanction for func_80049C24 may itself bear re-examination. User policy: 'I don't want to allow cheats of any kind.' Function moves to blocked lane to continue search for a genuine pure-C coalescing lever; not permanently parked.

remaining_gap.summary: First p's pointer addu does not coalesce its offset temp into the dest register (target reuses v0 -> `addu v0,v0,a2`; ours allocates v1 -> `addu v1,v0,a2`), cascading to the two `lh` base registers (3 masked-differing insns). The second p coalesces correctly. Asymmetry traced to the `if((s16)prev_idx<0)` reload giving prev_idx a two-def (phi) live range vs next_idx's single def. Additionally a0/a2 hold (prev_idx, table) swapped vs target (masked out, but a real byte diff) and the s3-for-next_idx choice must come naturally for a 0-rule match.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: HEAD's 7 regfix-subst rules rename exactly these registers (a0<->a2, dest v0<->v1) plus the s3 pin. Removing all of them requires GCC to naturally produce the target allocation, which the explored C forms do not achieve.

next_hypotheses: Targeted permuter run from candidate.c base to find the coalescing form (caveat: text1b.c is the highest-cascade file; ~50% apply-failure rate per docs/PERMUTER_PIPELINE.md and permuter-closability-evaluated memory).
ALLOCDBG / -da dump probe to see why the first p's offset temp is not coalesced into the pointer dest while the second's is.
Try forcing prev_idx final value through a single def (e.g. compute reload result then a single assignment) so its live range matches next_idx's single-def shape.

REJECTED 2026-06-22 by user decision after layer-2 cheat-reviewer FAIL: (a) the broad split-init-accumulation sanction's SOTN evidence base did not survive verification (semantic-asymmetry misread of e_coffin.c, in-loop false-positive in dra/6BF64.c, semantic-purpose misread of e_bone_ark.c, naming-role failure of sp20), (b) the EXACT form was already rejected by layer-1 cheat-reviewer on 2026-06-16, and (c) the 2026-06-13 narrow sanction precedent (func_80049C24) may itself bear re-examination. Future workers MUST NOT re-attempt this form. The mechanism (refs-count bump on same-variable accumulation) is the kind of GCC-internals-only justification that the project's cheat-by-any-spelling posture forbids in the absence of clear SOTN-master evidence — which 2026-06-22 verification showed the project does not actually have for this specific construct.
